untranslatable_reason: The signal requires monitoring the advance/decline line (A/D line), which is a market breadth indicator tracking NYSE advances vs declines. This indicator is not in the ALLOWED INDICATORS list. The core logic—detecting divergence between market averages and A/D line participation, and comparing A/D peaks to price peaks across time—cannot be expressed using only price, volume, and oscillator indicators. A/D line is a separate data stream requiring explicit breadth data unavailable in the provided indicator set.
translation_notes: Signal is regime-context-dependent (exit longs, avoid shorts). Entry conditions reference A/D line divergence detection which requires custom breadth data. Exit condition is to liquidate all long positions upon signal confirmation, but the confirmation itself depends on untranslatable A/D divergence metrics. Parameters specify divergence_threshold_days=2 and a_d_lag_tolerance_percent=0.5, neither of which can be computed from allowed indicators.
